summary: A high‑energy 1984 show packed with rare Stranger, Cumberland Blues and Gloria shines on a generally excellent soundboard despite some digi‑noise glitches.
review: The performance is widely praised for its adventurous set list – second‑set rarities like Stranger and Cumberland Blues, plus standout renditions of Gloria, Do It In The Road and Franklin’s Tower. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s killer solo, Brent’s trippy keyboards and Phil’s tight bass, while the soundboard capture is described as crisp and full‑bodied, though a few note digital noise on disc 3 and occasional skips after Space.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (9/11) give 4‑5 stars and highlight exceptional musical moments; recording is mostly praised with only isolated technical complaints, leading to a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, full‑bodied soundboard overall. Some reviewers report digi‑noise on disc 3 and occasional skips after Space, suggesting minor digital artifacts but no major mix problems.

standout_songs: Why Don't We Do It In The Road
poor_songs: Why Don't We Do It In The Road (in one review)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The setlist is noted for its oddball covers and rare second‑set choices, creating a wildly creative and high‑energy flow that many fans describe as classic 1984 Dead magic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers call his solo "killer" and his tone perfect, despite one comment about his personal struggles not affecting the performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass lines are described as "sonic and perfect sounding," anchoring the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: Slide work on guitar is praised as "rooster‑esque" and fitting nicely into the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work gets high marks for trippy synth/B‑3 textures, especially on Friend of the Devil.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drums space is highlighted as a strong, tripping segment; overall drumming is praised.
